intake.user_request: Create a workorder to fix the console.log → logger migration problem.

classification.plan_type_rationale: Rule 3: touches_hint spans 47 files across >1 top-level module directory (scanner/, pipeline/, plugins/, adapter/, semantic/, etc.). Also Rule 1 candidate: logger utility is a public utility module.

warnings: CLI files (src/cli/*.ts) intentionally excluded from scope — console.log in CLI entry points is user-facing output, not internal logging.

in_scope: All non-CLI src/**/*.ts files that use console.log, console.error, or console.warn (47 files, 198 calls)
src/utils/logger.ts — verify API surface is sufficient (info, warn, error, debug)
tsc --noEmit after each phase to verify no type regressions
out_of_scope: src/cli/*.ts — console output in CLI entry points is intentional user-facing UX, not internal logging. Separate workorder required if CLI logging standardization is desired.
src/**/__tests__/*.ts — test files may use console.log for debug output; not in scope
Changing logger API surface or adding new log levels
Log aggregation tooling or runtime log configuration changes

constraints: tsc --noEmit must pass after every phase (zero errors)
vitest must pass (1567+ tests) after every phase
No functional behavior change — only import + call-site substitution
logger import path must be correct relative to each file's depth
console.log calls in CLI files must NOT be touched

errors: semantic_scan: skipped — not required for strict migration workorder with enumerated candidates
